title: TPM_RC_SIGNATURE decoded as Ok(false) not Err
description: EccKey::verify maps TPM return code 155 (TPM_RC_SIGNATURE) to Ok(false) rather than Err. TPM_RC_SIGNATURE means the signature was structurally valid but cryptographically incorrect -- a legitimate negative verification result, not a transport or protocol error. If mapped to Err, callers cannot distinguish 'bad signature' from 'TPM communication failure'. The three-way split -- Ok(true) verified, Ok(false) not-verified, Err transport/protocol error -- is the correct API shape for a verify function. Alternative rejected: returning Err(SignatureInvalid) as a distinct variant forces callers to unwrap a Result just to get a bool, which is awkward for the common case.
